title: Police probe donations to Britain’s far-right Reform party
summary: Reform UK's finances draw police scrutiny as Farage fights to reclaim his seat in a by-election.
imageAlt: Police probe donations to Britain’s far-right Reform party
aiSummary: Reform UK's finances draw police scrutiny as Farage fights to reclaim his seat in a by-election.
keyPoints: Police probe donations to Britain’s far-right Reform party
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de internacional.

multimedia.alt: Police probe donations to Britain’s far-right Reform party
multimedia.caption: Police probe donations to Britain’s far-right Reform party

fullArticle:
British police are investigating at least 500,000 pounds ($670,000) in donations made to the far-right Reform UK party, adding to a widening campaign-finance scandal that prompted party leader Nigel Farage to abruptly resign his seat in parliament this week.

Police said on Friday that they were investigating potential offences under laws governing party donations, which could include concealment of the true source of funding or providing false information to a party treasurer.

Detectives are scrutinising two 250,000-pound ($335,500) donations made ahead of the 2024 general election by Fiona Cottrell, the mother of George Cottrell, a convicted felon and long-term financier of Farage’s political activities.
